findings.evidence: `rg` shows the generic API at `GaloisGroup.lean:214` and `GaloisGroup.lean:223`, while the new file only defines `galoisGroupEquivSqrtPrimes` at `PrimeGaloisGroup.lean:58` and has no `galoisGroupEquivSqrtPrimes_apply`/`_symm` lemmas.
findings.fix: Add prime-specialized simp lemmas analogous to `galoisGroupEquiv_apply` and `galoisGroupEquiv_symm_apply_gen`, e.g. characterizing its value as `Multiplicative.ofAdd (signPattern (fun i => Real.sqrt (p i)) σ)` and its inverse sending each generator to `(-1)^(ε i).val * gen ... i`.
findings.issue: `galoisGroupEquivSqrtPrimes` has no public `@[simp]` apply or inverse-on-generator lemmas, so downstream users cannot use the prime-indexed equivalence as a sign-change API without unfolding it back to `galoisGroupEquiv`.

summary: The prime-specialized Galois equivalence is exposed without the characteristic lemmas needed to use it as the sign-change isomorphism promised by the roadmap.
